hard.next_step: 6.18.26 KK - had initial discovery. Limited scope focused on spend, no budget for this year. Executing NDA as next step to discuss how much spend and if we can expand into additional modules 6.8.26 KK - waiting on presales assignment
hard.ais_why:
• MedImpact is at Qualified with only Merlin scoped and no discovery description recorded.
• The Agentic-platform-discussed flag is not set and there are no pain points or AI-requirement captured.
• Evidence is limited to a spend + supplier interest signal, insufficient to place AI appetite above exploratory.

north_star_verdict.headline: Fresh new-logo Qualified deal: MedImpact reached out after Gartner, ran an initial discovery on 18 Jun, and executed the mutual NDA on 29 Jun. Scope is spend-only ($120K) with no 2026 budget, but the June 2027 close date gives full runway to align to the 2027 cycle. The live risks are early-funnel, not fatal: single main contact (Linda Neidre), no economic buyer or champion mapped, and a business case not yet quantified.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-06-04, 32 days old at sweep (2026-07-06). Moved Initial Interest to Qualified on 2026-06-30. 346 days to a 2027-06-17 close. No date slips recorded. Last activity 2026-06-29 (executed NDA), 7 days ago, so engagement is live. Pace to a far-out enterprise close is comfortable; the constraint is depth of qualification, not speed.
evidence: Buyer-initiated: Linda Neidre emailed after Gartner on 2026-06-04 asking for a walkthrough of Zycus alongside Oracle for spend classification, savings visibility, and supplier analytics
Mutual NDA fully executed 2026-06-29 (Anya Dammermann, Contract Manager), a concrete contracting artifact reached in under a month
Buyer stated 'no budget for this year' per Next_Step 2026-06-18; the June 2027 close aligns the deal to the 2027 budget cycle rather than signalling a stall
Single-threaded on procurement/legal contacts (Linda Neidre, Anya Dammermann, Philip Lagela); no economic buyer, decision maker, or champion identified
Scope is spend-only iSupplier plus Merlin at $120K; expansion into additional modules is discussed but uncommitted per Next_Step 2026-06-18

metrics.narrative: MedImpact has articulated qualitative goals but no numbers. Linda Neidre's 2026-06-04 email names the outcomes they care about: improving 'spend classification, savings visibility, and supplier analytics' by using Zycus alongside Oracle. That is a clear directional value theme (spend visibility and supplier insight) but no dollar figure, spend-under-management total, supplier count, cycle time, or savings target has been stated, and Next_Step 2026-06-18 records 'limited scope focused on spend, no budget for this year.' The move is to use the post-NDA scope call to quantify: annual spend under management, number of suppliers, and the savings or efficiency figure that would justify 2027 funding.
sources: Task/email 2026-06-04 (Linda Neidre): interest in improving spend classification, savings visibility, and supplier analytics
Next_Step 2026-06-18: 'Limited scope focused on spend, no budget for this year'

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is identified or engaged. The visible contacts are Linda Neidre (Principal, Procurement Office), Anya Dammermann (Contract Manager, handling the NDA), and Philip Lagela, all procurement or legal roles, none with stated budget authority. Next_Step 2026-06-18 states 'no budget for this year,' pointing to a 2027 procurement budget, but names no CFO, CPO, or VP of Procurement who controls that budget. For a $120K spend-analytics plus Merlin deal at an enterprise healthcare payer, the economic buyer is typically a VP or C-level procurement or finance leader. The move is to ask Linda, on the post-NDA scope call, who owns the 2027 spend-management budget and to secure a direct introduction.
sources: Opportunity Contact Roles: none on record
Email 2026-06-18 (Linda Neidre signature): Principal, Procurement Office

decision_criteria.narrative: No formal decision criteria have been captured. The scope is documented (iSupplier for spend analytics, Merlin for AI-driven supplier insight) and Linda's 2026-06-04 note frames the interest around spend classification, savings visibility, and supplier analytics running alongside Oracle, which hints at co-existence-with-Oracle as an implicit criterion. But there is no RFI, RFP, BRD, scorecard, or proof-of-concept plan on record, and the deal is only 32 days old and just Qualified, so criteria have not yet been formalized. The move is to ask, on the scope call, how MedImpact will evaluate a solution and what a successful walkthrough alongside Oracle must show.
sources: Task/email 2026-06-04 (Linda Neidre): Zycus alongside Oracle for spend classification and supplier analytics

decision_process.narrative: The buying process is largely unknown but a legal/contracting lane is visible. The sequence so far reads: post-Gartner inbound (2026-06-04) to Linda Neidre, an initial discovery on 2026-06-18, then a mutual NDA handed to the relevant stakeholder Dammermann and fully executed 2026-06-29. So MedImpact routes vendor engagement through a formal Procurement Office and a Contracts function before deeper scoping. What is not yet mapped is the approval chain for 2027 spend, whether an InfoSec or compliance review will run (likely for a healthcare payer), and who signs off. The move is to have Linda walk through the buying steps and gates from scope confirmation to a 2027 funding decision.
sources: Email thread 2026-06-18 to 2026-06-29: NDA routed via Anya Dammermann (Contract Manager) and executed
Next_Step 2026-06-18: NDA as the next step before scope expansion

paper_process.narrative: Contracting has started at the first gate: the mutual NDA was requested by Linda Neidre on 2026-06-18, routed to the relevant stakeholder Dammermann, reviewed and approved by Zycus legal, and returned fully executed on 2026-06-29. That is a positive early signal (a functioning Procurement Office and Contracts team). The rest of the paper path is unknown: whether MedImpact requires a standard MSA and SOW, whether an InfoSec or HIPAA-adjacent security review applies (probable for a healthcare payer), and how procurement issues purchase approval for 2027 spend. The move is to confirm the full contracting path (MSA, SOW, security review) during the scope discussion.
sources: Emails 2026-06-18 to 2026-06-29 (Linda Neidre, Anya Dammermann): NDA requested, reviewed, executed

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is directional, not yet sharply articulated. Linda Neidre's 2026-06-04 outreach frames the need around 'spend classification, savings visibility, and supplier analytics' used alongside Oracle, which implies gaps in spend visibility and supplier insight on top of their Oracle stack. But no operational trigger (an audit finding, a supplier failure, a cost-reduction mandate), no pain owner beyond Linda, and no consequence-of-inaction has been stated, and Next_Step 2026-06-18 confirms only 'limited scope focused on spend.' The move is to ask on the scope call what is driving the need now and what breaks if MedImpact waits past the 2026 no-budget window.
sources: Task/email 2026-06-04 (Linda Neidre): spend classification, savings visibility, supplier analytics alongside Oracle

champion.narrative: No champion has emerged. Linda Neidre (Principal, Procurement Office) is the most engaged contact: she initiated the post-Gartner outreach, requested the NDA, and drove the initial discovery, which shows genuine sponsorship of the evaluation. But she has not been shown to have access to the 2027 budget owner, has not introduced other stakeholders, and there is no evidence she is selling Zycus internally against alternatives, so she reads as an engaged coach rather than a proven champion. Anya Dammermann and Philip Lagela are legal/contracting contacts, not advocates. The move is to develop Linda into a champion by asking her who else cares about spend visibility and who controls the 2027 budget, and to secure introductions.
sources: Emails 2026-06-04 to 2026-06-18 (Linda Neidre): initiated outreach, requested NDA, drove initial discovery
Opportunity Contact Roles: empty (no champion role assigned)

competition.narrative: No competitor has been named in any source: the Salesforce Competitor field is empty and no rival appears in Next_Step, tasks, emails, or the executed NDA thread. The only named third-party system is Oracle, which MedImpact runs today and wants Zycus to work 'alongside' (per Linda's 2026-06-04 email), so Oracle is the co-existing ERP, not a procurement-suite competitor being displaced. It is unknown whether this is a sole-source exploration or an early-stage evaluation that will add vendors later, and the real near-term threat is inertia: with no 2026 budget, MedImpact could keep managing spend on Oracle and defer, a no-decision risk rather than a loss to a rival. The move is to ask directly on the scope call whether they are evaluating other vendors or exploring Zycus sole-source, and to position Merlin's AI spend/supplier intelligence as the wedge on top of Oracle.
sources: Task/email 2026-06-04 (Linda Neidre): Zycus 'alongside Oracle' (Oracle is the incumbent ERP, not a suite competitor)

deal_movement.summary: Live, early-stage movement over the last month: buyer-initiated inbound after Gartner, an initial discovery on 18 Jun, stage advanced to Qualified, and a mutual NDA fully executed on 29 Jun. Momentum is real but shallow (spend-only scope, no 2026 budget, single main contact).

items.change: MedImpact reached out after Gartner requesting a Zycus-alongside-Oracle walkthrough for spend and supplier analytics
Initial discovery call held; scope confirmed as spend-only with no 2026 budget and possible module expansion
Mutual NDA fully executed by Contract Manager Anya Dammermann
Stage advanced Initial Interest to Qualified; Division reassigned to New Markets

competitive_position.summary: No competitor is named in any source as of 2026-06-30; the Salesforce Competitor field is empty and none surfaced in the emails, discovery notes, or NDA thread. Oracle is the incumbent ERP MedImpact runs today and wants Zycus to work alongside (Linda Neidre, 2026-06-04), not a procurement-suite rival being displaced. The strongest current threat is a no-decision / status-quo outcome: with no 2026 budget, MedImpact could keep managing spend on Oracle and defer past the 2027 cycle. Whether this becomes a competitive evaluation (typical healthcare-payer rivals would be Coupa, SAP Ariba, GEP, Ivalua, Jaggaer) is unknown and must be confirmed on the scope call before assuming sole-source.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: The one stated expectation is that Zycus complements, not replaces, their Oracle stack, improving spend classification, savings visibility, and supplier analytics on top of it. We are aligned in principle (iSupplier plus Merlin address exactly this) but the fit is unvalidated without a scoped walkthrough.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage win-strategy levers: get off a single contact and onto the 2027 budget owner, convert exploratory interest into a quantified case, and pin down whether this is competitive before investing presales.
flags: Multi-thread beyond Linda Neidre: ask her to introduce the 2027 spend-management budget owner (VP Procurement / Finance) so an economic buyer is engaged before the funding cycle
Convert directional interest into a quantified business case on the post-NDA scope call: annual spend under management, supplier count, and the savings figure that justifies 2027 funding
Confirm whether MedImpact is exploring Zycus sole-source or will run a competitive evaluation, and position Merlin's AI spend/supplier intelligence as the wedge on top of Oracle

The deal is single-threaded on procurement/legal contacts and 32 days old; developing Linda and multi-threading to power is the priority.

Merlin's AI spend classification and supplier intelligence is the natural wedge on top of Oracle and is currently under-positioned.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS is 4.0 (Moderate). Merlin is scoped and the buyer's stated goals (spend classification, supplier analytics) are AI-amenable, but no call evidence confirms AI appetite yet and the agentic story has not been discussed, so the signal sits at exploratory.

critical_signals.text: The 18 June initial discovery confirmed a spend-only scope with no 2026 budget, leaving module expansion as the upside to develop.
Contract Manager Anya Dammermann joined on the NDA and returned it fully executed on 29 June, but she is a legal contact, not a budget owner.
There is no 2026 budget and no quantified savings case yet, so the $120K spend-only price is not justified and hinges on the 2027 cycle.
No competitor has surfaced and the main threat is a status-quo decision to keep running spend on Oracle rather than a rival vendor.

scope_change.to: Spend (iSupplier) + Merlin, expansion to additional modules discussed
scope_change.detail: Scope started spend-only by the buyer's framing on 2026-06-18, not narrowed from a prior larger scope; expansion into additional modules is the stated upside, not a reduction.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer-initiated after Gartner and executed the mutual NDA, but framed interest as an exploratory walkthrough of Zycus alongside Oracle with no Zycus-specific preference or competitive comparison stated (Linda Neidre, 2026-06-04).

business_case.evidence: Qualitative goals only, spend classification, savings visibility, and supplier analytics; no spend-under-management, supplier count, savings figure, or ROI quantified (Linda Neidre, 2026-06-04; Next_Step 2026-06-18).

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A fresh, buyer-initiated new-logo deal at MedImpact: Linda Neidre reached out post-Gartner and executed the NDA in under a month, but it is spend-only with no 2026 budget, no economic buyer mapped, and no quantified case, so the win read is early and shallow rather than strong.

win_position.text: Early and shallow: MedImpact is engaged and NDA-executed, but it is a $120K spend-only Qualified deal with no economic buyer, champion, or quantified business case, so the position rests almost entirely on one procurement contact.
The buyer initiated the deal after Gartner and drove the NDA to full execution on 29 June, so intent is genuine rather than rep-manufactured (Linda Neidre, Anya Dammermann).
The buyer stated 'no budget for this year' and the 2027 budget owner is unmapped, so the whole deal hinges on securing 2027 funding through a person we have not met (Next_Step, 18 Jun).
No competitor is named and the real threat is a no-decision, keeping spend on Oracle, which we cannot counter until the business case is quantified.

deal_momentum.text: Real recent movement: post-Gartner inbound on 4 Jun, initial discovery on 18 Jun, stage to Qualified and NDA fully executed by 30 Jun.
The buyer pulled the next step, requesting the Oracle-alongside walkthrough herself, and legal engaged early via the executed mutual NDA.
Momentum is early-funnel only: no senior stakeholder has joined, no concrete decision dates were named, and the scope remains spend-only.

customer_commitment.text: MedImpact committed effort to a mutual NDA and returned it fully executed on 29 June, a concrete cooperative step beyond a generic info request.
Commitment stops at exploration: no budget is allocated for 2026 and no economic buyer has sponsored the evaluation, so there is no funding commitment yet.

deal_risk.text: Single-threaded on procurement and legal contacts (Linda Neidre, Anya Dammermann, Philip Lagela) with no champion and no power role engaged.
No-decision risk dominates: with no 2026 budget MedImpact can keep running spend on Oracle and defer past the 2027 cycle.
The value case is unquantified, so the $120K price is not yet defensible to a 2027 budget owner.

champion_strength.evidence: Engaged coach only (Linda Neidre); no champion with access to power (Contact Roles empty).

economic_buyer.evidence: No EB mapped; 'no budget for this year' with 2027 budget owner unknown (Next_Step 18 Jun).

business_case.evidence: Qualitative goals only, no quantified savings or spend figure (Linda Neidre 4 Jun).

contributions.evidence: customer requested next meeting (from call evidence).

commentary.win_position: Win 18 is carried by momentum 60 vs stage-expected 50; preference strength +0.40 (weight 20). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 60 (moving forward): commercial topics entering (from call evidence). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 0: no break-risk was observed in the swept evidence. Coverage is thin, so read this as 'nothing seen yet,' not 'nothing there.'
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 10 is anchored on the stage win (18), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 60, and dragged by risk 0. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
